title: Finding the Perimeter of a Rectangle

steps.instruction: Perimeter is the distance around the outside of a shape.
steps.work: A rectangle is 9 cm long and 4 cm wide.
steps.explanation: Imagine walking around the edge — the total distance is the perimeter.

steps.instruction: A rectangle has 2 lengths and 2 widths.
steps.explanation: Opposite sides of a rectangle are equal.
The perimeter of this rectangle is 26 cm.

title: Finding a Missing Side from Perimeter

steps.instruction: A rectangle has a perimeter of 20 inches. The length is 7 inches. Find the width.
steps.explanation: We know the total distance around and one dimension.

steps.instruction: Two sides are length 7. Add them: 7 + 7 = 14.
steps.explanation: A rectangle has two sides of each measurement.

steps.instruction: Subtract the known sides from the perimeter.
steps.work: 20 − 14 = 6 inches left for the two widths.
steps.explanation: The remaining perimeter is shared by the two width sides.

steps.instruction: Divide by 2 since both widths are equal.
